original_hypothesis.title: 100% Yield Fee Causes Permanent DoS of Liquidation
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: If the Vault owner sets `yieldFeePercentage` to `FEE_PRECISION` (1e9, representing 100%), the `liquidate` function will revert due to a division by zero when calculating the yield fee. This permanently halts the conversion of yield to prizes.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: `_setYieldFeePercentage` uses `>` instead of `>=` when checking against `FEE_PRECISION`, allowing it to be exactly 100%. The math in `liquidate` subtracts this from `FEE_PRECISION` in the denominator.
deployment_assumptions: Owner mistakenly or maliciously sets yield fee to 1e9

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Setting fee to 100% breaks core functionality

operational_questions: Is there any valid reason to set the fee to 100%?

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Transaction reverts with division by zero
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Yield cannot be converted to prizes, breaking the protocol loop

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Complete halt of prize funding for the vault

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
